steps.title: Rewrite the second term using the sign rule
steps.content:
The term $-12y(-2x + 3y)$ can be rewritten:

$$-12y(-2x + 3y) = 12y(2x - 3y)$$

So the expression becomes:
$$(x - 11y)(2x - 3y) + 12y(2x - 3y)$$

steps.title: Factor out the common binomial
steps.content:
Both terms share the factor $(2x - 3y)$:

$$(x - 11y)(2x - 3y) + 12y(2x - 3y) = (x - 11y + 12y)(2x - 3y) = (x + y)(2x - 3y)$$

steps.title: Expand using FOIL
steps.content:
Apply FOIL to $(x + y)(2x - 3y)$:

$$(x)(2x) + (x)(-3y) + (y)(2x) + (y)(-3y) = 2x^2 - 3xy + 2xy - 3y^2 = 2x^2 - xy - 3y^2$$

That matches choice B — B is correct.
